paragraph: Language barrier is a figurative phrase used primarily to refer to linguistic barriers to communication, which became particularly evident during the 2020 Covid19 pandemic. As the Internet community becomes global, language barriers play a fundamental role in how we process information. As testified by Confirmation bias, individuals appear to prefer receiving information in the language they are familiar with. It takes extra energy to understand the meaning of a message in a foreigner language, especially beyond superficial understanding. Consequently, it’s common for readers to isolate themselves from information in foreign languages. For example, English-speaking users feel quite intimidated when they are using chinese social media like Weibo. Weibo is one of the main media in Chinese-speaking world, it functions both as social media and a news outlet for official media. At present, the amount of monthly active users in Weibo has reached 516 million, most of the users receive news from the hot topic posts.24 hashtags and users’ Despite being no Firewall, information on Weibo has

paragraph: 15 day”35, From the book, Neil also brings out a content called ”the news of the describing the fact that new communications technologies have fragmented the information to suit the fragmented conversations between people. Indeed, the fragmented information loses the completeness logically, and will decrease content’s accuracy. In terms of conversation and discourse, Neil Postman compares the television to the conversation carrier. Within the carrier, the truth becomes related to the techniques of communication subjectively. As Neil Postman indicated,”A major new medium changes the structure of communications by creating new forms of truth-telling.” Thus, the carrier or the platform of the content takes significant roles in truth dissemination. For example, in the print era, truth-telling and public discourse was following logical and serious principles. However, the new technology commercialized the discourse to separate context from meanings.

paragraph: In sum, with the fragmented information decontextualized by television and other new media now, “the news of the day” represents a message without coherence and logic. Under this climate, how to design new platforms to strengthen the public discourse power is a possible method to defend fragmented and amusing information.

author: Xinyue Nie

paragraph: 16 The Mechanism of Serendipity Inspirations could be found in the article“Designing for Unexpected Encounters Experience.”36 with Digital Product: Case Studies of Serendipity as Felt In the article, the writer Rung-Huei Liang discussed how random mechanisms have been employed in the user experience in the digital products. In terms of the “aesthetics of interaction design”, serendipity provides more involvement, experience, and surprise to satisfy users' needs that usability and functionality could not apply. For example, users will choose the random mode when playing music on the player page to gain a sense of novelty even with familiar contents. Rung-Huei Liang stresses that serendipity is not only the consequence of the digital numerous content, but also an essential experiential quality in interaction design. By serendipity, the user would feel involved in the content, and will have a tendency to explore the relationship of unrelated information. Serendipity belongs to implicit interaction to connect unrelated content, which could be applied into the news feed mechanism to break the viewer’s filter bubble.

paragraph: News Flashlight is a 60-days project including a satire website and a publication output to explore fake news visual language. The project uses a newspaper layout containing 60 fake news reporting styles. The project lasted for 8 weeks. From the research, I conclude and categorize the templates of typical fake news visual language as different topics, such as “source agnostic”,” Half real, half fake”, ”Breaking headline”, and etc. The 60 projects are shaped by those topics and News apply the template into some latest news then. Thus, the vibe of the Flashlight is ironic and satirical. Applying critical and creative thinking is a method to understand, visualize, and describe the complexity of fake news to reduce them.

author: Xinyue Nie
thesis_title: Post-Truth Era, Polarization, and Discourse by Serendipity
paragraph: 19 DAILY NEWS is a cultural probe used to research a target audience's perception of fake news in a daily context. The project aims to understand the users’ subjective attitudes on controversial topics. Emoji and degrees of recognition reflect essential parts of viewers’ attitudes on fake news topics. Whether the choice of emoji and comments from one person on the same topic reflects the consistency of their attitude or not is the research goal. The participants are asked to fill out two questionnaires that describe one topic content in different expressions. Then, the participants are asked to use emoji and the number which represent the degrees of recognition to fill their comments separately. The topic is the quantitative, their reflections are the variables. Showing interesting results, the research output indicates that people using emojis with vague meanings display less consistency on their attitudes.

paragraph: Fragmented Reflection is a speculative design idea for an interactive exhibition exploring confirmation bias and isolation. The installation is constructed by a circular mirror folding upward to the structure and portraying content on the top of it. Participants perceive the content/image through the mirror, creating their own narrative from the fragmented part. This is the metaphor of the post-truth era, in which people are convinced by the fragment of a whole issue.

paragraph: As serendipity becomes the keyword of my capstone project, SEEMS explores how to generate discussions among users from different backgrounds and aims to decrease the polarizing tendency of digital environments. Fake news is one of the most disputed issues in online discussions. Personalized news feed and personal confirmation bias, transform opinions into potential conflict, rather than supporting an open dialogue. Dialogue is needed to construct deliberative democracy. Spaces for open dialogue need to build on respectful climate, enabling individuals to interact with a larger group of people, and thus Architecture encountering diverse perspectives. By creating an of Serendipity the discussion can be fostered. Architecture of Serendipity is a system or common space to encourage people to mingle together. “Serendipity is also seen as a potential design principle for

thesis_title: Post-Truth Era, Polarization, and Discourse by Serendipity
paragraph: This project explores how to apply the idea of serendipity in a design artifact that distances itself from personalized content and platforms. It aims to stimulate the player’s habit to break their echo chamber and be interested in others perspectives. The project imagines a design instantiation of the principle of deliberative democracy.

author: Xinyue Nie
thesis_title: Post-Truth Era, Polarization, and Discourse by Serendipity
paragraph: SEEMS is a multiple-ending interactive video. Inspired by “Black Mirror: Bandersnatch,”4142 SEEMS is a choose-your-own-adventure story in which the users uncover the endings for themselves. The interactive video presents parallel endings that depend on participants’ choice. In the video, the structure branches the plot and lets the audience decide the narrative on their own. The viewer creates their own narratives based on their bias, interests, and emotions. They have the chance to restart the story and explore other paths. author: Tong Sha
thesis_title: The Disproportionate Influence Of Online Reviews
paragraph: In an environment full of business promotion and Influencer economy, how does design affect on the online community to give each user the same influence and generate trusted user-content? How to deal with the disproportionate influence of internet users?

author: Tong Sha
thesis_title: The Disproportionate Influence Of Online Reviews
paragraph: Today, more and more people rely on the review platform to help them make decisions. For example, when buying things, people often look at ratings and comments on Amazon; when they want to find a place to eat, people also check restaurant ratings on Yelp and Google Map; influencers' "unboxing" videos on Youtube and Instagram also have very high views. Studies have shown that online reviews may heavily influence people's decision-making. However, based on the study, reviews written by different users have different exposure rates, and the disproportionate influence provides fertile ground for internet marketing and fake reviews. On Yelp, users can sort reviews by “user voting” and “written by Yelp Elites”. In this case, compared to those long careful-edited reviews with high votings or written by higher-level members, a lot of common users’ reviews are often not seen and read. On social media platforms like Instagram and Youtube, influencers’ reviews have high exposure, but their product reviews usually are driven partly by interest. As the industry's default rule, Influencers’ product reviews are priced: promotion fees for an Instagramer are $100 per 10,000 followers. In this case, there is a grey area between real insights and paid advertisements. This allows online reviews to have exaggerated and fake parts, and they are not neutral and trusted anymore. Thus, how to keep those user-generated reviews truthful and trusted? How to minimize the impact of business promotion and internet marketing on reviews? These situations suggest common users need a new platform to write and read reviews, and it can help equalize the disproportionate influence among different users. Thus, there is no need to build a hierarchy system in the platform. It needs a justified algorithm to filter out business promotion as well as fake reviews.

author: Tong Sha
thesis_title: The Disproportionate Influence Of Online Reviews
paragraph: In today's society, people are affected by the review culture in many ways. Research shows that there are hierarchies on some review platforms. Hierarchy creates different exposure rates. Compared with some advanced user reviews, ordinary people's opinions are more easily ignored. Similar problems also appear on social media: the influence and exposure rate of Internet celebrities is much higher than that of ordinary people. Therefore, the commercial promotion has become a means of livelihood for some Internet celebrities, and consumers often cannot identify advertisements and real opinions. Imagine an online community without the "follow/be followed" function: everyone's opinions are equally important, no ranking, no opinion leaders and influencers, and no commercial involvement. Therefore, everyone can express their true opinions. Based on this concept, this article attempts to design a trusted review platform without a hierarchy. At first, the article made a related experimental design around the network scoring mechanism. With further research and references to the literature on blockchain and decentralization, the research question evolved into how to give each user an equal influence. The final project is about the interface design of the review platform, which includes the interface, introduction brochure, and narrative.

author: Tong Sha
thesis_title: The Disproportionate Influence Of Online Reviews
paragraph: 1. The evolving form and target of online reviews In 1979, Zagat uses pamphlet to record reviews for local restaurants, hotels, movies and so on. According to Burton’s article, ratings and reviews on Zagat guidebook are based on thousands of people. In 2011, Zagat was acquired by Google. The reviews collected by Zagat are visible on Google Maps. Lately, The Zagat 2020 survey is reprinted. This guide book lists the top restaurants in NYC from three different aspects. Nowadays, the review system has been established mostly on online platforms. the spread of mass media promotes the exposure and update frequency. In 2004, Yelp.com was born. It is a large review website. Users can score one to five stars for local businesses on the site. Its appearance represents the popularity of the online review platform. One year later, Google Maps and YouTube appeared. Google Map's review function is almost the same as yelp: users can rate one to five stars and leave a message. On YouTube, people use videos to record their "unboxing" reactions. To this day, video reviews are still popular. From research on commonsensemedia.org, “Unboxing videos show the product in a favorable light and can have the same effect as advertising”, that is, merchants will pay KOLs or influencers to “review” their products. The two parties became a commercial partnership. This phenomenon usually happens on popular channels. Besides, the target of the review is constantly changing. From the beginning of the restaurant evaluation to the appearance of Peeple. The object of the review is the change from object to human. In this process, some applications have also implemented human reviews to some extent. For example, Uber rates drivers and passengers, takeaway apps rate food delivery services, Ratemyprofessor.com rates professors, and so on. This further shows that the impact of reviews on our lives is growing. It covers almost every aspect of life. According to research, 93% of people will act after reading a positive review, and over 80% of people trust online reviews as much as personal recommendations (Doward, 2011). Therefore, it is very important to create a truthful and reliable review environment.

Internet celebrity marketing companies will choose potential Internet celebrities to sign contracts. At the same time, marketing companies will interface with some commercial brand companies to help online influencers receive advertising. As a result, both parties can make a profit. According to one of the co- founders of an influencer marketing company, Joe Gagliese, his company is making 4.5 million dollars annually, and the number keeps going up. This article also reveals that the company's contracted influencers’ revenue can reach $ 30,000-100,000. Gagliese also pointed out that the promotion ability of Internet celebrities even surpassed athletes and movie stars because of their high interaction with their fans, and they are often trusted by fans (Lieber, 2018).

author: Tong Sha
thesis_title: The Disproportionate Influence Of Online Reviews
paragraph: 1. Video collage-Peeple x Black Mirror Nosedive When making this video, the theme of the article was about the mechanism of the online review platform. As mentioned above, Peeple is a review platform born in 2015. The subject of the review is people. It reminds me of an episode in Black Mirror: the protagonist lives in a world where people rate each other. To purchase a high-end residential house, the protagonist must raise her rating. She first consulted a "marketing" company and then did a lot of work. Unfortunately, her score plummeted in the end. Eventually, she went to prison. In prison, she was finally freed without having to bother with the score. This video combines a promotional video of Peeple and a TV series. It depicts the future of evaluating people with irony. If we digitize the relationship between people and classify them with levels, how many people will collapse on the road to high scores like the protagonist? (video link: https://drive.google.com/open? id=1DXLxqRW3klblh3BTRzfAwzllIDbKp7iv) 2. Experiential design- a more nuanced way than five stars During the cultural probe stage on Transformation class, I was still discovering new ways to improve the online review interface. One issue I found about the online review interface is the over-simplification. On some social platforms, People only get limited choices to express their feelings, like the binary like and dislike buttons on dating apps, five-star scales on different attributes of hotels on Airbnb. Thus, this project explored how to invite people to make more nuanced evaluations instead of rate or star things.

author: Tong Sha
thesis_title: The Disproportionate Influence Of Online Reviews
paragraph: 3. Ethnography research around Pratt During the ethnography research, I interviewed nine pratt students. First, I asked them which restaurants around pratt they have recently visited. Then I asked them how they heard about this restaurant at first. Most people told me that they were introduced by friends or classmates. Another part of them told me that they search for restaurants through review platforms such as Yelp and Xiaohongshu, which is a well-known Chinese review platform. According to our conversation, I recorded the key information of the interviewee on the mind map.

author: Tong Sha
thesis_title: The Disproportionate Influence Of Online Reviews
paragraph: Among them, the dialogue of three students was very enlightening to my topic. Two students mentioned Xiaohongshu. After that, I did a case study on Xiaohongshu. According to the information, Xiaohongshu is a review platform as well as the eCommerce platform in China. On Xiaohongshu, users can take photos and upload reviews. Different from other platforms, web celebrities, Kol and even stars have overwhelming influence and discourse power on Xiaohongshu. When you visit the site, recommendations from Internet celebrities appear on the home page. Usually, these reviews are directly sponsored by merchants. Also, users can buy products directly from Xiaohongshu. So the platform itself will cooperate with influencers to advertise its products. According to this case study, I developed two issues on review platforms: Exaggerated or unreal information brought by influencers, and reviews from common users can not have an equal chance to be noticed and read. These two findings laid the foundation for my thesis question.

author: Tong Sha
thesis_title: The Disproportionate Influence Of Online Reviews
paragraph: 4. Immersive box- visualization of online reviews As the research progressed, I noticed that different people on the platform have different influences and voices. As mentioned in the previous research on influencer marketing,

author: Tong Sha
thesis_title: The Disproportionate Influence Of Online Reviews
paragraph: influencers have an overwhelming discourse advantage on social media and review websites. In this project, my idea is to visualize the impact of reviews on people. In this "box", different curtains are covering the object in the center of the room. The object represents things being reviewed. When people stand in different places in the room, translucent curtains will form different filters to block the object. At the same time, according to the different identities of reviewers, the size and texture of curtains are also very different: The curtains that represent the celebrity review may be very large and thick. In contrast, the narrow and thin curtains represent the views of ordinary users on review sites. Some curtains are soft to the touch and represent an easily accepted point of

author: Tong Sha
thesis_title: The Disproportionate Influence Of Online Reviews
paragraph: view. Some curtains are made of water, which makes people not want to be close, representing some irrational speech in online reviews.

author: Tong Sha
thesis_title: The Disproportionate Influence Of Online Reviews
paragraph: Ever since social websites like Yelp, YouTube and other review-based platforms became popular, the review and rating culture is influencing social websites users in real life. Many Internet users indulge in scoring and rating for products, services, and even humans. Also, they read reviews and check ratings created by other users. On those review platforms, different users can have a different power of influence. This article is going to discuss the disproportionate influence among users, especially between ordinary users and influencers, and explore its consequences. Based on the concept of the utopian vision for the Web, this article tries to create a truthful and trusted review-based platform where each user can get equal power of influence.

author: Tong Sha
thesis_title: The Disproportionate Influence Of Online Reviews
paragraph: The hierarchy system in the online community leads to unequal influence between users. On the other hand, the birth of web celebrity also intensifies the disproportionate influence, which gave birth to influencer marketing. Furthermore, capital involvement blurs the line between true review and advertising. Therefore, the most fundamental solution is to give each user equal influence and voice. This idea is very similar to the utopian vision for the World Wide Web. According to the founder of the World Wide Web Tim Berners-Lee, the original model of the Web was a platform without hierarchy, where everyone can share and contribute information. In recent years, the popularity of blockchain technology and the concept of decentralization have confirmed people's desire for equal communication and the removal of capital participation. Tim Berners-Lee is developing a new app, Inrupt, to return the right to access and process every bit of data to each user.

author: Tong Sha
thesis_title: The Disproportionate Influence Of Online Reviews
paragraph: Yelp elite According to Matthew Van Deventer’s research, there is a hierarchy on one of the most famous review websites, Yelp, and they are called elite members of Yelp. “Users can be nominated or self-nominated to be an elite member. Elite members may be invited to private parties thrown by businesses, special offers, and other perks”. As for the way to become elite members, Yelp will choose them “based on several criteria and aim to accept Yelpers who show potential for being stewards in their community”(Deventer, 2018). In a word, The platform holds the right to select specific users. These users can enjoy privileges not available to others. On the other hand, according to the description of Yelp's official website, users can sort reviews based on some characteristics when viewing reviews. Among them, users can choose “sort by Yelp Elites”. Then the reviews from elite members will appear on the top of the review section. This further indicates Yelp’s preferential treatment for the elite squad: more views and greater voice.

author: Tong Sha
thesis_title: The Disproportionate Influence Of Online Reviews
paragraph: Influencers on social media Based on Schouten’s investigation, influencers firstly gathered groups of followers on social media like Youtube and Instagram. The “attract millions of followers by sharing content curated from their daily lives”. After that, they will sign a contract with marketing companies and seek endorsement with brands and retailers (Schouten, 2019). In the field of influencer marketing, Joe Gagliese, one of the co-founders of an internet celebrity marketing company, revealed that social media influencers even had a more powerful impact on common customers than celebrities. He said, “We believe influencers are more impactful than athletes and TV stars because they are more relatable and so their audience is more tapped in” (Lieber, 2018). Gagliese also suggests that successful influencer endorsement needs to describe the product in as objective a tone as possible, rather than simply posting a product image online. “If you were good at the job, you’d talk about the watch and engage with the audience by telling them about its pros and cons, why you’d buy it, and who you’d buy it for” (Lieber, 2018). Thus, Merchant- sponsored promotions can be published online in the form of reviews, and it is hard for customers to recognize the real insights and paid advertising.

author: Tong Sha
thesis_title: The Disproportionate Influence Of Online Reviews
paragraph: Utopian vision for the Internet According to Ossolo’s article, Tim Berners-Lee, the creator of the World Wide Web, was to build a non-hierarchical platform where information can be freely shared. He called the vision “utopian”. His original idea is similar to the philosophy of Wikipedia and Github, “which are devoted to collaboration and information sharing” (Ossolo, 2019). The development of the Internet runs counter to his original intention. Take today's review platforms and social media as examples, sponsored content will appear in the most prominent place on the site. As mentioned above, online celebrities also use their influence to advertise brands. As a result, people often cannot discern true information.

author: Tong Sha
thesis_title: The Disproportionate Influence Of Online Reviews
paragraph: Blockchain and decentralization Based on Zijing Xu’s study, The most significant feature of the blockchain is decentralization. Each block holds complete information (Xu, 2018). Therefore, an attacker cannot find an entry point for stealing information; people can access all the information through many nodes. Recent study shows, similar to blockchain technology, Tim Berners-Lee, the father of the World Wide Web, is designing an app for decentralization, Inrupt. It aims to “decentralize the web and take back power from the forces that have profited from centralizing it. In other words, it’s game on for Facebook, Google, Amazon.” Through this app, users can quickly access all the data about themselves. Examples include schedules, music playlists, health data, property status, and more (Brooker, 2018).

author: Tong Sha
thesis_title: The Disproportionate Influence Of Online Reviews
paragraph: Another app related to the concept of decentralization is called Crocomments. This Chinese app has created a review platform. In the platform, the test scores are divided into four categories: food and accommodation, goods, services, and entertainment. This platform gathers various types of assessments and eliminates the involvement of third- party websites. At the same time, this is an app for mutual communication between acquaintances, to a certain extent, to avoid false comments brought by commercial propaganda.

author: Tong Sha
thesis_title: The Disproportionate Influence Of Online Reviews
paragraph: Rationale Today, more and more people rely on online reviews to help make their decisions. For common reviewers, one prevailing issue on an existing review platform is the unequal chance of being noticed and read based on the hierarchy of users. Another one is there might be an exaggerated or fake part of influencers’ reviews and users cannot recognize it.

paragraph: My intention of this project is to create a non-hierarchical and trusted review platform: every user’s opinion has an equal chance to be read, and it decreases the possibility of exaggeration and untruthfulness brought by interest. In this platform, consumers will no longer see strangers' comments. The system displays the ratings of people who users know in real life, or users can select people he/she knows as long as their reviews are open to the public. The system forms users' unique social circle through the collection of users' personal information, such as graduate school, social media and address book. Reviews from those people will float randomly in the interface. In the interface, each circle with different patterns randomly represents different users. author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: For my thesis, I argue that the framework of alchemy can be a critical design tool for understanding the current relationship between people and authority through the artifacts or behaviors of contemporary taboos. Furthermore, I believe alchemy oers insight for transformation within oneself. Therefore, my thesis aims to present new visions of class struggle at this current moment, and to facilitate the singling out of certain audiences.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: Alchemy is a primary science that explores the essence of subjects. It begins with analysis which enables us to investigate a subject holistically, followed by the process of recombining the ingredients or dierent parts of the subject into a new form, and ends with a coded and allegorical documentation system to encrypt the alchemical process. According to Roger Bacon, a medieval English philosopher and Franciscan friar, "Alchemy is a science simply composed of one and by one, naturally conjoining things more precious, by knowledge and eect, and converting them by a natural mixing into a better kind (Bacon 5)."

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: Contemporary taboos are ethically controversial and may contain artifacts or behaviors that have been banned or restricted by authorities, that have faded or been neglected by history or artifacts or that have been misperceived, so that the original messages have collapsed and become new taboos. Through holistic investigation, appropriate transformation and allegorical projection, I believe Alchemy could serve to reveal the applications and appreciations of contemporary taboos.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: In the book Alchemy: The Great Secret, Andrea Aromatico , an expert of "Hermeticism", discusses the concept, theory and practice of alchemy based on its history. Instead of studying Alchemy in a neutral perspective like Andrea, I employ my alchemic practices in design fields and self-explorations. If the value of designers is to build connections, the connections built by alchemists break the boundary of scientific disciplines and bridge the gap between macro and microcosms. This has the potential to reveal the relationship between allegorical information and the perception of audiences in the current design field. Below I give a brief summary and analysis of how the purpose, practice and symbolism of alchemy could lead and shape my research based on insights provided by the above text.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: The goal of alchemy was always the conversion of metal into a purer form and the conversion of the practitioner into a purer self. In order to achieve the "purer" condition, alchemical practices always include finding (1) prima materia. To find the animating material at the core of all life, and to command the mechanism whereby it worked. (2) The Alchemical Vessel. A literal and figurative problem that alchemists confronted was to find a container capable of receiving the prima materia and preventing its escape. The vessel of "The Great Work" (the ultimate achievement of alchemy) may be understood not only as a literal alembic or retort in a laboratory, but also symbolically, as the crucible in which the soul of the knowledge seeker is transformed. (3) The philosopher's stone. The philosopher's stone was understood not as mere gold itself, but as a substance capable of transforming the very nature of matter, and of sustaining life. According to Rupecissa, "The matter of the stone is a thing of little worth, which may be found anywhere (DeVun 49)." Morienus arms that "matter is unique and everywhere rich and poor possess it; it is unknown to all; it is before the eyes of everyone; it is despised by the vulgar, who sell it cheaply like mud, but the philosopher who understands it holds it as precious (Patai 163)." These references reveal the spiritual aspect more than its literal identity. "By studying the world of metals and discovering its secrets, human beings arrived at knowledge of both the individual self and by extension, the whole (Aromatico 42)." In other words, the logic in digesting all things, which were considered "the universal mind," is the massive knowledge system that alchemists are trying to seek. However, the universal mind itself could hardly be considered the ultimate achievement beyond a relatively neutral perspective. In terms of individual practices and exploring procreation of the "Elixir" for any imperfect existences, the procedures to follow and the allegories to record are required for alchemists to research and practice.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: The famous Alexandrian alchemist Zosimos described the purpose of alchemy as the perfection of the alchemist such that they could come into contact with the divinity within themselves (Gupta 4). The working process to accomplish the purpose, is called "The Great Work". The Great Work was much more than a mere collection of procedures. The first step was to read the works of the past masters, to search for lost, obscure, and precious treatises, acquire them attentively, with the special interpretive perceptiveness that alchemy demanded. All in all, the Great Work meant the act of examining the world with new eyes.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: First, consider theoretical knowledge as basic. The process of practicing the Great Work was divided into four stages. Each of the four stages had its own importance, its own allegorical and literal meanings, and its own Latinate name, based on the color it appears: the Nigredo (black), Albedo (white), Citrinitas (yellow) and Rubedo (red). The stages by which a substance was gradually transformed followed one or two general methods, the "dry way" or the "wet way". Within either of the two ways, the Great Work was further divided into three distinct phases, each of which depended on the three basic substances, salt, mercury, and sulfur. For the purpose of starting my alchemic experiments for purer form of my studies and myself, further case studies are required.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: The experiment consists of the purpose, the process and the environment of the alchemic practices. "The space is well-ventilated and immaculate, set in a propitious location, chosen for its ability to attract the vital fluids of nature, its spiritual emanations, and the cosmic rays of the sun and moon. This is the sancta sanctorum of the alchemist (Aromatico 78)."

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: The archives and treatises of alchemical progress are documented in a coded and allegorical symbolism. Alchemy imposes a unique ethical commitment upon its disciples: the alchemist is enjoined to be both charitable and envious - that is, to share the science's material gifts and wisdom, but also to prevent the vulgarization of its principles and techniques through indiscriminate disclosure of its art. The language of alchemy rarely gives us a single meaning for any term. This is because what it designates is never seen from a single point of view, but as a function of the role, by turns element, principle, subject, or object, it may play in the work. The ideology could be practiced through layers and depth of visual communications in terms of both art and design. Besides illustrating allegorical documents, allegorical writing also has been practiced in alchemy. The 18th century alchemist Salomon Trismosin oers an example of which companion to the mysterious images: "Among these contradictions and apparent lies we find the truth, as among these thorns we plunk the mysterious rose. We cannot enter into the rich garden of the Hesperides to see the golden tree and pick the precious fruits without killing the dragon that always watches over it and defends the entry (Splendor Solis at The Alchemy Website)." This example shows the core approach to sharing alchemical content, which constructs an allegory frame detailed with individual representations of the target role and its function of the research object.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: The symbolisms that are used to document the alchemical process could be considered sacred for the specific purpose of hiding information from abuse. Yet how can we explore the potential values as a tool for form making?

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: Roald Homann, a Nobel Laureate in chemistry, creates? word portraits of chemists and prose poems to celebrate the wonder he unceasingly finds in his field. Homan's eort here suggests that the curiosity-squashing damage wrought by rote drills of the periodic table could be redeemed by the sense of awe and far-reaching appreciation embraced in these writings. In this book, Homann traces back to the origin of chemistry, lists some basic properties of molecules and compares chemistry logics and ancient perspectives together, breaking the boundary between the occult and science. I consider this text valuable for its narrative approaches and aesthetic practices.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: For thousands of years, humans have been trying to see to the center of things. Alchemy emerged with the growing curiosity about the composition of objects, of living creatures and of the world. As a chemistry novice and designer, this book allows me to rethink much of my acquired knowledge and develop imagination, as the title of the book suggests. Homann mentions that some ideologies of alchemy have been proven wrong by modern science, such as the existence of the philosopher's stone, yet the methodologies and ideologies that were developed through the practices, generation by generation, directly led to the cultivation of chemistry.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: Second, in what ways might chemistry encounter the practice of alchemy: Homann describes chemistry as "the science of molecules" and knowing without seeing is the heart of chemistry. Even though we know now that molecules are not the smallest composition of things, they still build an entire advanced system of describing the interactions in our world even though we cannot visually witness them. Alchemy, on the other hand, started with investigating all the creations of the world, and has never been trapped by a single form like a molecule. In this perspective, alchemy has way more potential value than chemistry. Due to the restrictions of equipment, environment and resources, the independent alchemical practices had moved towards dead ends. But as modern researchers and activists, holding the intention of transmuting subjects into purer forms provides further possibilities of alchemical study. To consider new ways of perceiving at the molecular level, modern chemistry creates so many fascinating approaches to reshape and reconnect our comprehension of the microcosm. Take the infrared absorption spectrum for instance, lights shine on an object; where its frequencies match the characteristic tones and overtones, light is absorbed. A dierent shape, stronger or weaker bonds, a dierent molecule - all of these would lead to absorption of dierent colors - a dierent timbre. In short, certain advanced technology developments pave the way for new perceptions on the component of things, which might reverse the formal alchemical understandings or even raise whole new levels of alchemy revival.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: Lastly, I would like to talk about the universal potential of promoting alchemical symbolisms. The symbols were widely used by medieval alchemists in the form of illustrations, and? letters. Underneath the symbolic language a set of chemical transformations took place. These were used not only for protecting the progress of alchemical practice from being abused by the masses, but the symbolism also enabled the descriptions on any matter to be more accurate. The experiences of individuals shape the bonds between oneself and objects. Think of how our imaginations easily get carried away by existing physical objects— it is almost impossible for us to understand a single matter neutrally and thoroughly without being influenced by memories. However, the symbolisms have the ability to avoid the above condition. By highlighting the connections and presenting analogs, the representation of a thing could actually break the boundaries of multiple ranges. The alchemical symbols provide us the opportunity to describe or receive certain messages of a subject through the functions of each part.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: With an initial insight of the alchemical philosophy and its basic practice, I would like to fully apply my research into dierent aspects of alchemy for further experiments and research directions. As the foundation of Medieval Western European alchemy, the Emerald Tablet spread to date even though the physical object has been lost in history. The texts carved on the emerald stone were translated into multiple languages and are still being interpreted by alchemists. Before I step into the content and the interpretations of the Emerald Tablet, I would like to take a look at its history and legend.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: Thoth (or Hermes), who was considered as the author of the Emerald Tablet of 36,000 years ago, was an Atlantean priest-king that traveled to ancient Egypt where he left the original tablet in the safeguard of the Pyramids of Giza after inscribing the information for others to discover. According to the legends, the Tablet holds profound secrets from ancient times that are still relevant today. For instance, the Emerald Tablet holds the key to reaching new levels of spiritual and physical awareness within its cryptic message, laying out a hidden formula for achieving these literal and symbolic transformations. These messages have been influential throughout history, including shaping the practices and philosophies of western alchemy, and a massive impact on Hermeticism.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: Sir Isaac Newton, one of the greatest minds in human history, had his own translation and interpretation of the Tablet. In it he highlighted the information in the Emerald text that was still important centuries after the initial translations appeared. Some say reading it a hundred times over had a profound impact on the soul. The wording itself isn't the important information, but rather how one interprets it, and the impact it will have on one's life.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: As an alchemist who is eager for the knowledge contained in alchemical practice, my own initial interpretation of the Emerald Tablet formed through repetitive reading and thinking. Massive content is contained within such a brief text, beginning with the universal mind of adapting everything into one system of understanding. This is followed by the 4 fundamental Greek elements of the world, which are fire, water, air and dirt which are represented by the sun, moon, wind and earth. The prophet shows up next. By acting as a philosopher's stone and playing the alchemist, the prophet has the ability to turn anything into purer forms. Through analyzing the analogies inside this tablet, I view the messages as a separate narrative layer. In literal communications, Hermes, the author of the tablet, illustrates the world creation process and the almighty power of "the father of all perfections", which I understand as the philosopher's stone. At the same time, the text is a guidance for us to practice the alchemical experiments of mimicking the formation of the world, which, in a way, is the process of pursuing the philosopher's stone. Lastly, what I consider the most important part is the overall summary of the practice of Internal Alchemy. After understanding the formation of the world and bridging layers of gaps between macrocosm and microcosm through alchemy, one would finally become the philosopher or the father of all perfection, accomplishing the ultimate goal of alchemy, and shaping the world into a new form. In general, I assume the spiritual and physical awareness that the Emerald Tablet is trying to promote is to present the structure of forging the philosopher's stone, and also how to become one.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: Tao Te Ching is the philosophical work of Laozi dated in the Spring and Autumn Period, divided into two parts. The "Tao jing" verse is in the first place with thirty-seventh chapters, the "Te jing" verse follows after with forty-four chapters. Based on the foundation of "Tao Te", Tao Te Ching discusses the ways of self-cultivation, governance, military use, and health, and is also known as the king of all dogmas. Tao Te Ching is one of the greatest masterpieces in Chinese history. According to the statistics of UNESCO (United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization), Tao Te Ching is the most famous cultural publication that has been translated into foreign languages except the Holy Bible.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: Look into the content of the two chapters in Tao Te Ching. "Tao" refers to the truth of the existence of the universe, the truth of the natural world. "Tao" here is also the principle that each living individual needs to follow for self-cultivation. "Te" refers to the special world view, methodology and methods for people who live in the world.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: Starting with the value of Tao Te Ching, Laozi promotes the way to cultivate nature by respecting the principles of nature and following the rules of nature. The "natural" here means literally everything in the world. The idea of "natural Taoism" is used in the governance of the state, that is, inaction. In this case, inaction is defined as not doing anything and using minimal management behavior to get the biggest eect. There must be natural wisdom that manages the human nature of the manager..

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: Next comes the personality of Tao Te Ching, which is like water, good for all things, not contending, modest and downwards. The best is the noble person. If you can do the character like water, you are a noble person. Laozi says this is because water is beneficial to everything but does not compete for anything. Where there is water flowing from the river, there's an oasis and plants are lush. Wherever the water flows, there's life. That is to say, water is very willing to give, but never fight for their own interests. On the other hand, the waters of the rivers flow without a fight, but objectively they also get a return from everything. Each tree is a cistern. Where the grass is lush, then the water is evaporated into the sky,and returns to the ground through rain. What happens after the rain? This river is not dry, it forms a benign cycle. Therefore, Lao Tzu said that "The saints do not accumulate, they oer to people, the more they oer, the more they accumulate." That is to say, the saints never accumulate wealth, rather they try to do good things for others, and then they will have it themselves. The more you oer things to others, the more you will accumulate.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: Finally, the wisdom of Tao Te Ching. 主 柔 守 弱: Most of us know that strong is better than weak, but Laozi promotes the idea from another angle that weak produces strong. 光 ⽽ 不 耀: When you are in your most glorious moment of life, don't show yourself o, otherwise it will turn problematic. ⼤ 智 若 愚: On the surface, it may seem to be stupid ， but it is actually a great wise man. People with great wisdom don't care too much about small things, and big things are very clear. 上 德 若 ⾕: The minds of the most noble and moral are as broad as the big valley. A person must have a broad mind, tolerance is a virtue. 不 ⾔ 之 教: Speak with caution.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: As one of the greatest alchemy books, The Mirror of Alchemy manages to stick a massive amount of knowledge into a small space. This book is ascribed to Roger Bacon without being written by him. This work is more literal than most of his works which are full of either spiritual references or arcane references. Instead, this text secularizes the process of alchemy in a more medicinal or chemical sense to delineate the type of furnace, the type of fire, and the type of material to choose, which is extremely rare among alchemical texts which are often veiled by awkward symbolisms.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: "Nature, contains nature, nature overcomes nature, and nature meeting with her nature, exceedingly rejoices, and is changed into other natures." As the theory and principle of the whole practice, the quote reflects the logic of mimicking nature.. Even some methodological details and especially the projection steps of Elixir is more bizarre to interpret, but throughout the outcome(?)becomes tempting for me to taste that delightful thing, wherein the whole intention of the philosophers is placed.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: Through the definition, natural principles, procreation of minerals, the materials to choose, the manner of working, the quality of vessel and furnace, the color appearing and medicine projections, The Mirror of Alchemy explains almost all the required information and possibilities before and during alchemical working, which could be interpreted as an Alchemical experiment report under ideal conditions except the specific amount of ingredients.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: Start with the definition of alchemy, "for Hermes said of this Science: Alchemy is a Corporal Science simply composed of one and one, naturally conjoining things more precious, by knowledge and eect, and converting them by a natural commixtion into better kind. A certain other said: Alchemy is a Science, teaching how to transform any kind of metal into another: and that by a proper medicine, as it appeared in many Philosophers' books. Alchemy therefore is a science teaching how to make and compound a certain medicine, which is called Elixir, which when it is cast upon metals or imperfect bodies, does fully perfect them in the very projection (Bacon 11)."

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: "Where first it is to be noted that the natural principles in the mines are Argent-vive, and Sulphur. All metals and minerals, whereof there be sundry and diverse kinds, are begotten of these two: but I must tell you, that many accidents coming between, change the metals, as it is evidently to be seen in the diverse Philosophers books (Bacon 17)."

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: Material Ranges: - Minerals (gold, silver, steel, lead, copper, iron); - Vegetables (of which sort are herbs, trees, and whatsoever springs out of the earth); - Living creatures (of which sort is man's blood, hair, urine, excrements, hens' eggs and what else proceed from living creatures); - Middle minerals (of which sort are all kinds of Magnesia, Marchasites, of Tutia, Coppers, Allums, Baurach, Salts, and many other); - One of the seven spirits by itself (as Argent- vive, or Sulphur alone, or Argent-vive and one of the two Sulphurs, or Sulphur-vive, or Auripigment, or Citrine Arsenicum, or red alone).

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: "There must therefore be such a matter chosen, where in there is Argent-vive, clean, pure, clear, white and red, not fully complete, but equally and proportionally commit after a due manner with the like Sulphur, and congealed into a solid mass, that by our wisdom and discretion, and by our artificial fire, we may attain unto the uttermost cleanness of it, and the purity of the same, and bring it to that pass, that after the work ended, it might be a thousand and thousand times more strong and perfect, then the simple bodies themselves, decoct by their natural heat (Bacon 19)."

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: Equipment: - Furnace: we need to have such a furnace like unto the Mountains, not in greatness, but in continual heat, so that the fire put in, when it ascends, may find no vent; - Vessel: vessel must be round, with a small neck, made of glass or some earth, representing the nature or close knitting together of glass: the mouth whereof must be signed or sealed with a covering of the same matter, or with lute. (Aristotle says, in the light of lights, that Mercury is to be concocted in a three-fold vessel, and that the vessel must be of most hard Glass, or (which is better) of Earth possessing the nature of Glass.) (Note: the heat does not immediately touch the matter of Sulphur and Argent-vive, because the earth of the mountain comes everywhere between.)

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: "Though nature does perfect anything, yet she cannot thoroughly mundify, or perfect and purify it, because she simply works on that which she has. If therefore we should hard and scantily find fire working in them. And although we are not ignorant of the fire, yet could we not come to the thorough modification and perfection of it, by reason of his most firm knitting together, and natural composition (Bacon 24)."

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: Procedures and fire controls: 1. Mix everything to a due proportion (which no man knows); 2. Decocting it to coagulation into a solid lump; 3. Fire and Azot are sucient for you; 4. Heat perfect all things; 5. See, see, see but not weary; 6. Let your fire be gentle, and easy, which being always equal, may continue burning, and let it not increase; 7. Know you that in one thing, to wit, the stone, by one way, to wit, decoction, and in one vessel the whole mastery is performed; 8. Patiently and continually; 9. Grind it seven times; 10. It is ground with fire; 11. This work is very like the creation of man: for as the Infant in the beginning is nourished with light meats, but as the bones are strengthened with stronger: so this mastery also, first it must have an easy fire, whereby we must always work in every essence of decoction. (Note; though we always speak of a gentle fire, yet in truth, we think that in governing the work, the fire must always by little and little be increased and augmented unto the end.)

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: Phenomena (accidental and essential colors appearing in work): - Putrefaction, The stone is made black. (When you find it black, know that in that blackness whiteness is hidden, and you must extract the same from his most subtle blackness); - It was red, not with a true redness. (It is often red, and often of a citrine color, it often melts, and is often coagulated, before true whiteness); - It is also green. (Concoct it, till it appears green unto you, and that is the soul. And another, know, that in that, green his soul bears dominion); - The peacock color before whiteness. (Know you that all the colors in the world, or that may be imagined, appear before whiteness); - True whiteness. (When you shall find whiteness stop in the glass, be assured that in that whiteness, redness is hidden: and this you must extract, but concoct it while becomes all red); - A certain ashen color. (After whiteness, you cannot err, for increasing the fire, you shall come to an ash color); - True redness. (Do not set light by the ashes, for god shall give it to you molten, and then at the last the King is invested with a red crown by the will of God)

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: "A great and hidden secret: one part is to be mixed with a thousand of the next body, and let: all this be surely put into a fit vessel, and set it in a furnace of fixation, first with a lent fire, and afterwards increasing the fire for three days, till they be inseparably joined together, and this is a work of three days: then again and finally every part of any near body: and this is a work of one day, or one hour, or a moment, for which our wonderful God is eternally to be praised (Bacon 26)."

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: "The red Elixir turns into a citrine color infinitely and changes all metals into pure gold. And the white Elixir does infinitely whiten and brings every metal to perfect whiteness (Bacon 27)."

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: The origins of alchemy are lost in history and theories abound as to where it might have originated: - God taught Adam and later to Moses. - Fallen Angels taught it to human women in exchange for sex. - It is a remnant of lost Atlantean technology. - Extraterrestrials taught it to our ancestors. Whatever its true origin is, recorded history documents an esoteric tradition that has existed for thousands of years.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: In the Egyptian Mysteries, Man was composed of various spiritual and mental components as well as the physical component, and each had its proper "medicine." These Secret Sciences advanced over time, and tales of wondrous healing oils, life-giving potions, and imitations of gold and precious stones have survived even to our day.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: When Alexander the Great arrived in Egypt around 300 B.C.E., he fell in love with the whole culture, and the Egyptians welcomed him with open arms. In Egypt, Alexander initiated a sweeping campaign of construction and restoration, including the city named after hum - Alexandria. It is the Great Library that the Hermetic Philosophy and alchemy congealed as a Path to Spiritual Attainment, and its secrets were only revealed to initiates under an oath of alliances.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: By around 30 B.C.E., the Roman legions had swept the world, and the last of the Egyptian Ptolemies had fallen to Roman rule. During this insurgency, a very large part of the Great Library was destroyed by fire. Initially, Rome was tolerant of Egyptian ways. In fact, the worship of Isis spread well into the Roman world with temples in Rome itself.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: In 325 C.E., Rome ocially became Christian, and in 391 the Emperor Theodosius made heresy punishable by death and ordered the destruction of pagan temples. Most of those practicing the Hermetic Philosophy fled the country and migrated east to Arab lands not occupied by Rome.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: Beginning with the Islamic invasions around 800 C.E., knowledge of alchemy spread into Western Europe, largely through the works of Ibn Sina (also known as Avicenna.) In Medieval Europe, alchemy became very fashionable. By now, kings and rulers everywhere had heard of the wonders possible through alchemy, especially the turning of lead into gold.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: There were also a great number of cons and scams perpetrated by those who pretended to know the secrets of the alchemists. Many unsuspecting people lost their life savings in hopes of finding ways to inexhaustible wealth. Then, around 1310, Pope John XXII issued a decree of prohibiting the practice of alchemy, and gold-making in particular, with heavy fines against those who traded in alchemical gold.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: Paracelsus revolutionized the Alchemical Art and is considered to be one of the fathers of modern chemistry and pharmaceutical medicine. He was constantly at odds with the medical professionals of his day and was looked upon very suspiciously by the Church because of his views and opinions. Because of this, some believe Paracelsus was murdered in 1541. In a strange twist of irony, these helped lead to the end of the Age of Alchemy and the beginning of Chemistry as we know today.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: By the end of the 17th century, there was growing religious freedom, which sparked a wave of interest in all things Mystical. Alchemical texts became still more widely available, and scholars openly identified themselves as Rosicrucians, Adepts, or Alchemists. The spiritual aspects of alchemy appealed to many, apart from any practical work. Robert Boyle and Isaac Newton studied alchemy during this time. Newton was fully involved and produced volumes of work. In fact, he considered himself more of an alchemist than a physicist or mathematician. His notes indicate that he believed he was very close to success in the alchemical art of metallic transmutation.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: By the 1800s, the practice of Alchemy had largely disappeared in the outer world in favor of its still young oshoot - chemistry. Alchemy survived underground in various "Secret Societies," which became popular, especially towards the end of the nineteenth century.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: H. Spencer Lewis received a charter from some of these European contacts to form the Ancient Mystical Order of the Rosae Crucis, better known as AMORC. Among other things, they taught laboratory alchemy as it was handed down by earlier Rosicrucian sources.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: In the early 1940s, one student of these classes was Albert Reidel. Frater Albertus went on to teach these classes himself and then split o to establish the Paracelsus Research Society in 1960, which became accredited as Paracelsus College in the early 80s.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: With the passing of Frater Albertus in 1984, there seemed to be a void in alchemical teachings and a lack of a central point where students could exchange information. By the early nineties, through the eorts of several P.R.S. students, contact with the french group was made, and the Philosophers of Nature (PON) was formulated to fill the void with fresh ideas and to carry on research in alchemy (Bartlett 23).

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: Now we have the Internet - the new "Library of Alexandria." As we shall see, chemistry, left to grow unfettered, has nearly come full circle to rediscover the Hermetic Philosophy.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: Contemporary Taboos represent the artifacts or behaviors that have been banned or restricted by authorities, that have faded or neglected from history or artifacts or that have been twistedly perceived, and the original messages which have collapsed and become the new taboos. I chose this concept to challenge the cultural artifacts that have faded from history but aren't necessarily useless for the development of human civilizations. On the contrary, in terms of providing a sustainable cultural environment, some of the messages within these contemporary taboos might be essential to adjust the static thinking which is led by imperialist(?) cultures and ways of thinking, and share our knowledge of the world with all humankind. As an Alchemist, my purpose is to use Alchemy as a tool of investigating the essence of things to reveal the hidden valuable messages under the Contemporary Taboos and transform them into something that the mass public can perceive or even act upon, for the potential of challenges or revolutions, of oneself and of communities.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: Native Americans possess a relatively independent history apart from other civilizations of the world. The unique climates and landforms shaped alternative customs and linguistics. In the interpretation of history, many native American civilizations like Aztec culture are considered the unfit ones which cannot survive. Many of the languages and rituals are buried together with the fading cultures themselves. Codex Borgia is one of the Aztec ritual and divinatory manuscripts that have survived the Spanish conquest of Mexico. Together with a few other pre-Columbian documents, they were coined the 'Borgia group' to integrate their place of origin and their linguistic identities. Substantial portions of the codex convey various aspects and attributes of the 260-day ritual calendar. The more detailed ceremonial aspects of the images have been harder to understand. The codex itself is sacred and obscure for most, and the ritual calendar system is seemingly useless under the domination of the solar calendar on a global scale. However, an Alchemy practice over Codex Borgia (Alchemy of Tonalli) can not only overlap the Aztec ritual calendar onto the solar calendar and make the Aztec calendar feasible to everyone, but also provide brief introductions of the Aztec deities and aesthetic interpretations into an everyday level through our original understandings of solar calendar and the design tools like a Aztec typeface which is designed from the aztec arts and applied to serve Aztec introductions.

author: Yubo Ku
paragraph: Martial Arts, or Kung fu, originated in Ancient China as an internal practice system that was occupied with spiritual, mental or qi-related aspects, rather than just a combat system. Yet the development of Patriarchal society and China's cultural revolution have deeply aected the misinterpretations of Martial Arts. Even though the internal practice of Martial Arts like Qi Gong is not extinct, the wide acceptance of the external aspects of Martial Arts make the internal practice into a kind of taboo. An Alchemy practice of Martial arts (MOCHI system) uses artifacts other than physical forms like the game of Chinese chess to reveal the concept of internal practice and introduce it to Martial Arts practitioners.

paragraph: 3 Research Questions -How can design help with the understanding of weaponized light? -What message can the design of light weaponization convey / How it can address urgent social problems? -How can we integrate light weaponization with design and develop new styles and methodologies? Abstract Light illuminates the dark, provides comfort, furnishes energy to organisms, guides us to a path, and brings us hope. However, when the light is used for dominance, it can also be destructive. It can burn, blind, terrorize, and cause hallucinations or hinder our sleep. This thesis explores light for its significant presence and potentially destructive behavior to humans.

author: Chih-Yuan Chang
paragraph: What is light? Light, scientifically and theoretically described, is a type of energy created by transverse and electromagnetic radiation of a wavelength that can be distinguished by human eyes. Humans' lives are deeply embedded in the presence of light. Cubitt (2014) describes that light is such a mediation, not only between people, but between human and non-human worlds. Light fills and forms the world. For millennia light from the sun and the celestial bodies, and from burning organic matter derived from sunlight, spilled with the seemingly boundless generosity of a god or gods into the human universe. It bathed our skins and filled our eyes, nurtured our food and habitats, made it possible to see and be bewitched by beauty. Sources of light can generally be classified in natural light and artificial light. Natural light produces heat and color from the radiation filtered through the earth’s atmosphere from the sun. We encounter it in constant permutation and innumerous cosmic diversities: the sunshine, clouds, the twilight of the lower atmosphere, sunrises and sunsets, and also the unusual circumstances of the aurora borealis.

author: Chih-Yuan Chang

author: Chih-Yuan Chang
paragraph: Artificial light, different from nature in that it is human-made, can emit from sources such as candlelight, gaslight, and other typology of lamps. However, the term artificial lighting is often used to refer to light emanated by electric devices. We are exposed to countless artificial lights - interior lighting, street lamps, floodlights, traffic signals, advertisements, and urban decorations. It also displays and monitors with an emissive blue light that surrounds our daily lives. Artificial lights are typically easy to manipulate: the direction, focus, and color can be conveniently increased and decreased to achieve a particular configuration. This dynamic manipulation allows us to generate significant aesthetic and functional variations. The use of artificial lighting is crucial in agriculture and gardening, the life of indoor cultivation. Light sources come in many types - incandescent bulbs, halogen lamps, metal halides, fluorescent tubes, compact fluorescent lights, and LEDs - but commonly emit by passing an electric current through a filament or a pocket of inert gas. Both natural and artificial lights offer humans a considerable amount of benefits. Light gives people the ability to see, and its absorption or reflection by objects gives the perception of different colors. Light from the sun warms the Earth, stirs up global weather patterns, and initiates the life-sustaining process of photosynthesis. On the magnificent scale, light’s interactions with the matter have

author: Chih-Yuan Chang
paragraph: 5 helped shape the structure of the universe, as Erik Gregersen states in The Britannica Guide to Sound and Light. There are many health benefits of sun exposure: to support the skin production of vitamin D, increase serotonin helping people with anxiety and depression, and improve quality of sleep. When human skin is exposed to sunlight, it absorbs vitamin D, which is a vital nutrient to prevent bone loss, decrease the risk of heart disease, and reduce the risk of sun-related cancers. Sunlight reduces depression. Natural light not only reduces blood pressure, but also helps us defend against stress, anxiety, and Seasonal Affective Disorder (SAD). Third, natural light regulates human circadian rhythms and amends sleep cycles for more energy and better focus. Light induces a sense of hope; it makes things visible and spiritually illuminates life. For example, light allows us to see things clearly and positively, and it energizes us. In the Bible, light symbolizes righteousness, and God is light. God is holy with no sin, no taint of corruption, and inequity. The Bible mentions that if we do not have the light, we do not know God. Schwarz and Kirkpatrick (1994) state that idioms such as “bring to light” or “come to light” have the meaning of discovering or revealing. (P.203) Although the light has numerous positive influences on humans, it can also become profoundly damaging when used to the extreme. Like the old saying: “things will develop in the opposite direction when they become extreme.” The ultraviolet light can damage the skin; eyes could lose contrast or be blinded temporarily, and even worse, permanently visionless. Artificial light at night can cause headaches, work fatigue, increasing risks of depression and anxiety, sleep disorders, obesity, diabetes, cancers, and more. Kevin J. Gaston and others (2015) mention that artificial light gives humans extra time for activities. Lighting increases available time for economically productive manufactures, leisure, and recreational activities. Humans adhere to the rhythms of the natural cycle of day and night so that the disseminative use of artificial light disrupts us no longer experience nights completely. (p.14-23) The International Dark-Sky Association (2016) states that our bodies produce the hormone melatonin in response to the circadian rhythm. Melatonin benefits preserve us healthy. It has antioxidant properties, affects sleep, supports the immune system, reduces cholesterol, and improves the functioning of the thyroid, pancreas, testes,

author: Chih-Yuan Chang
paragraph: 6 and adrenal glands. Nighttime exposure to artificial light suppresses melatonin production. Frank H. Mahnke (1996) writes about light as a dominant component of the relationship between humans and their environment. It expands on the perspectives and disciplines of light and health from different fields of study, such as natural sciences, biology, technology, philosophy, medicine, psychology, and human factors engineering. In the eighth chapter, Biological Effects of Light, he states the importance of understanding the three spectral qualities of artificial light, how they affect color, and how the color, in turn, affects human eyes and feelings. They are: “Spectral Power Distribution,” which is the amount of power from a light source in each color band or spectral region; the “Color Rendering Index,” which was developed to describe how artificial light sources render well colors compared to natural light. And the “Color temperature” The term - the color temperature - was devised to explain the intensity of light emitted by a natural source. Researchers evaluated some of the biological findings and became more aware that light isn’t just an adjunct in seeing. Perhaps we should be warier of the varied kinds of artificial lights that we used than a medical point of view of how simulated fire can entirely be a substitute for natural light. (P.101) For more than a hundred billions of years, a light from the sun produced energy, which in turn gave animals, and humans oxygen, water, food, plants. Light controls all of life’s operations. Innumerous aspects of our mental and physical self are affected by the rhythms of natural light. The Earth rotates on its axis twenty-four hours a day, and within this amount of time, the light changes its color and intensity. There are three components of solar radiation. Each will impact human organisms, such as radiation on the skin and light through the eyes. The first is Ultraviolet, which produces Vitamin D and causes an Actinic effect. Ultraviolet, X-ray, and other radiations cause chemical changes to the skin and redden the skin. Then there is visible light radiation, which activates the pineal organ, invokes endocrine and autonomic effects, and entertains the circadian rhythms. Lastly, there is infrared radiation, which would heat the action on the skin and the body temperature, to worsen, even the pain. Light in Art, Design, and Photography Corrodi and Michelle (2008) notice that the world was divided into pairs of concepts in modernism’s schema of polarity, such as good-bad, bright–dark,

author: Chih-Yuan Chang
paragraph: 7 light-heavy, or transparent–secret, still influences our thinking today. Meanwhile, Lou Michael (1996) systematically describes the intimate connection between space and light in architecture. He narrates the interwoven relationship between illuminance and luminance, reflectance and reflections, light and shadows, texture, and surface. He argues that architects should “not design with light, design with the effects of light” - as the title illustrates. Light pertains to illumination, which causes the space to be visible. Jai McKenzie (2014), an artist and researcher based in Berlin, claimed that the history and future of photomedia are inherently linked to light. Her book explores the techno-historical development of photography and a future forecast of the influence of ingenuities and insights. She presents a timeline of light technologies from the early image machine to the digital image machines nowadays and into the future year of 2039, which will be two hundred years since the invention of photography. She speculates on the evolution of technology and its impact on the structure of photomedia - a term for the future of photography and image creation that embraces new media technologies, and also an abbreviation of two words’ photographic’ and ‘media.’ Believing that every future analysis is based on our understanding of contemporary tendencies and our utopian or dystopian perspectives of the future. In the future, when new paradigms are established, the history of photomedia will not be ignored or buried. Instead, it will not only generate abrupt issues and further questions about photomedia, but also advance the development of the technology and the proliferation of photomedia in art and design. McKenzie uses the example of photo-historian Geoffrey Batchen. The latter believes that photography will be exterminated in the future because a new technology for photomedia will arise and will replace all the visions and experiences of the past. Ryoji Ikeda, a Japanese visual artist and an electronic composer uses disturbing strobe light visualizations in his art pieces that engage scientific precision and mathematical aesthetics. He generates a sophisticated optical device, mixing sound and light. The light is projected on various surfaces and materials; the combination of light designed through mathematical rules and the ‘raw’ sound characteristic of his music produces an immersive installation that is hard to forget. He widened the perception of digital media and electronic arts. Visitors may experience seizure and discomfort due to the large-scale strobing light projection due to the glitches, hard edges, and patterns. In the F​ inancial Times​, Ikeda stated, “I like playing around with the thresholds of perception...If it’s too

author: Chih-Yuan Chang
paragraph: The Hungarian photographer and educator György Kepes utilized light to offer new sensory experiences and expand the range of perception. For György Kepes, 11 new optical technologies such as infrared and ultraviolet rays, microscopic and telescopic photography, X-rays and other radiation techniques were remarkable achievements that can further paintings, photographs, and image production. In the endeavor of photography, he investigated different methods, such as photograms, double exposure, and “photo-drawings” as new ways to use light. The photogram, a typical avant-garde technique, is a photographic image displayed by laying objects right onto the surface on a photosensitive element, without the use of a camera. Photograms are actual demonstrations of the power of light. The combination of ephemeral phenomena with geometric compositions plays an indispensable role in his work. (Keats, 2015)
